'''Mike Wainwright''' (born 25 February 1975) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the {{rlp|sr}}. He played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], the [[Leigh Centurions]] ([[List of Leigh Centurions players|Heritage No. 1300]]) in the [[RFL Championship]] and for the [[Swinton Lions]] in [[RFL League 1|National League Two]].<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Wainwright was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], [[England]].

==Career==
He has previously played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]].

Played for Woolston Rovers before joining Warrington's Academy, he was a halfback as a junior and played {{rlp|so}} in the 1993 Academy Cup Final victory, before switching to back row. He left the Warrington Wolves at the end of the [[1999 Super League season]] to join the Salford City Reds. He re-joined Warrington ahead of the [[2003 Super League season]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}

Following his career in rugby, Mike moved into financial services and is currently working as 'Head of Partnerships' for AFEX based in Manchester.

==International==
Wainwright toured Australia with the Great Britain Academy team in 1994. He was named in [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 40-man training squad in preparation for the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Larder leaves out Schofield |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-larder-leaves-out-schofield-1592364.html |website=The Independent |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=19 July 1995}}</ref> but was not included in the final squad.

Wainwright later chose to represent [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]], and was selected for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup|2000 World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Aussie is a World Cup Scot |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/933736.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=23 October 2024 |date=20 September 2000}}</ref>
